title: Globalization and Local Cultures
interdisciplinary_options: African Studies and the African Diaspora
description: This course introduces students to how diverse cultural groups are shaped by and respond to global processes such as economic integration, political change, migration, and digital technology. Students will understand globalization from an anthropological perspective.

title: Peoples of Latin America
description: The culture of different groups in Latin America with special attention to indigenous cultures and contemporary problems.

title: Culture in Africa
interdisciplinary_options: African Studies and the African Diaspora

title: Contemporary Cultures of the Middle East
description: Contemporary cultures of the Middle East.

title: Introduction to Global Studies
interdisciplinary_options: African Studies and the African Diaspora
description: This course serves as an introduction to the interdisciplinary field of global studies. It provides an overview of the major disciplines represented in the program, introduces the main regions covered in the program, and discusses important global issues.

title: Capstone in Global Studies: Directed Readings
prerequisites: Course requires permission of instructor; GLST will enroll approved students directly (no self-enrollment or non-GLST advisors); Course is open only to junior and senior majors and minors

title: States and Firms
description: This course focuses on the relationship between multinational corporations and the state. It explores the origin of firms, as well as issues such as corruption, tax policy, bailouts, and economic development.

title: Technological Change and Society
description: This course explores how changes in technology affect society in four broad areas: its impact on how we communicate, on the workplace, on regulatory issues surrounding technology firms, and on humanity itself.

title: Global Civil Society and Social Movements
description: This course focuses on global civil society groups (both advocacy groups or apolitical) and on social movements that seek to fight injustices.

title: Globalization and Environmental Sustainability
description: This course introduces students to social, political and economic factors that have contributed to globalization, development and their impact on the environment.

title: International Development
description: This course provides an interdisciplinary overview of the international development system and issues related to development in those countries variously referred to as third world, developing, underdeveloped, or peripheral countries.

title: Internship in Global Studies
prerequisites: Open to all GLST major and minors but requires instructor permission to enroll
interdisciplinary_options: African Studies and the African Diaspora

title: Topics in Global Studies
description: This is a variable topics course. Specific topics vary from semester to semester.

title: Culture, Power and Identity: Western Ideas & Institutions to 17th Century
description: This course traces the early development of Western ideas and institutions from the ancient Near East; Greece and Rome; the medieval civilization(s); to the European Renaissance and Reformation.

title: Culture, Power and Identity: Western Ideas & Institutions from 17th Century
description: This course traces the development of Western ideas and institutions and their global impact from the seventeenth century to the present.

title: American Pluralism
description: This course is an introduction to the origins, development and structure of the United States as a pluralistic and multiracial society from 1609 to the present.

title: Global History Since 1500
description: This course deals with the emergence of the modern world, including such topics as the expansion and intensification of cross-cultural interaction; imperialism, colonialism, and nationalism.

title: East Asian History: Themes & Issues
prerequisites: HIST 101, HIST 102, HIST 103, HIST 104, or equivalent

title: Modern Chinese History
description: The course is specifically designed for foreign students studying in Beijing.

title: Survey of Islamic History
prerequisites: HIST 101, HIST 102, HIST 103, HIST 104, or equivalent
interdisciplinary_options: African Studies and the African Diaspora

title: Latin American History: Themes & Issues
prerequisites: HIST 101, HIST 102, HIST 103, HIST 104, or equivalent
interdisciplinary_options: African Studies and the African Diaspora

title: US History to 1865: Themes & Issues
prerequisites: HIST 101, HIST 102, HIST 103, HIST 104, or equivalent

title: US History since 1865: Themes & Issues
prerequisites: HIST 101, HIST 102, HIST 103, HIST 104, or equivalent

title: African History: Themes & Issues
prerequisites: HIST 101, HIST 102, HIST 103, HIST 104, or equivalent
interdisciplinary_options: African Studies and the African Diaspora

title: Modern German Culture and Ideas I
description: This course will investigate intellectual and cultural responses to major events of nineteenth-century German history.

title: Modern German Culture and Ideas II
description: This course will cover the major phases of modern German history.

title: The Silk Roads: Global Trade, Culture, & Politics
description: The course covers exchanges between nomad and sedentary populations in East Asia and the flourishing of information routes.

title: Violence Drug Trafficking & Crime in Latin America
description: The aim of this course is to examine the historical factors behind Latin America's contemporary security crisis.

title: Topics in History
description: Special topics or new approaches of current interest to the instructor.

title: Modern France: From Empire to Postcolonial Nation
description: Modern France from the Revolution to the present.

title: The British Empire: From Barbados to Brexit
Ireland: Colony to Nation State
description: Ireland from colony to nation state.

title: Empire, Sex, Violence: European Colonial History
Modern European Nationalism: Myth and Reality
Europe in the Age of Catastrophe, 1900-1945
Food, Hunger and Power in the Modern World
description: Food and power in the modern world.

title: The Holocaust: History and Memory
description: History and memory of the Holocaust.

title: Rise & Fall of Soviet Union
description: Rise and fall of the Soviet Union.

title: African History Post-1600
interdisciplinary_options: African Studies and the African Diaspora

title: African Music's Influence on American Music
interdisciplinary_options: African Studies and the African Diaspora
description: African music's influence on American music.

title: The Caribbean & Central America in Colonial/Modern Times
The Mexican Revolution in Popular Imagination
Political Theory
prerequisites: PHIL 130 or equivalent; please check requirements for

title: American Politics
prerequisites: ANTH 100, PLSC 102, PSYC 100, SOCL 101, WSGS 101, or

title: International Relations in an Age of Globalization
interdisciplinary_options: African Studies and the African Diaspora

title: Comparative Politics
prerequisites: ANTH 100, PLSC 102, PSYC 100, SOCL 101, WSGS 101, or

title: Speak Up Democracy
description: "Speak Up Democracy" is a course on perennial questions about the workings of democracies. Participating students will investigate the connection between speech and political power, ruminating on whose voices are heard (and silenced) in a democracy. This summer program is animated by a central question: how should we speak to one another in a democracy? The course is open to high school students participating in the summer program.

title: Law and Civil Rights
description: The course focuses on the political processes surrounding laws and civil rights.

title: Mock Trial
description: Students will study the trial as a process for finding truth and administering justice by learning the dynamics of the trial, by developing trial strategy, by learning how to conduct and respond to direct and cross examination and by delivering effective opening and closing arguments. Mock trial is an engaged learning course. Prior Permission is required.

title: Moot Court
description: Students will study the appeal as a process for finding truth and administering justice by learning the dynamics of appellate advocacy, by developing oral argument strategies, by learning how to respond to questions posed by judges in competition, and by delivering effective summations and rebuttal arguments. Moot court is an engaged learning course.

title: Conflict Management
description: This course considers a variety of different approaches to international conflict management: direct bargaining, mediation, military intervention, economic sanctions, arbitration, adjudication, and peacekeeping. In addition to learning about the differences among these various conflict resolution techniques.

title: Representation in the United States
description: Considers what "political representation" means and how scholars study how well representation works in the contemporary United States.

title: International Human Rights
description: This course focuses on the theoretical foundations of such international human rights as well as their practical implications. It discusses the roles of states, intergovernmental organizations, and nongovernmental organizations in shaping human rights norms and practices. They will also understand practical causes and consequences of human rights policies.

title: Politics of Marginalized Groups
description: This course focuses on the role that race, ethnicity, and marginalized status plays in American politics and public policy. It will provide a general overview of several groups, including Blacks, Latinxs, Asian and Muslim Americans, Women, LGBTQ, immigrants and refugees, indigenous peoples and other groups.

title: Political Numbers
description: This course will train participants to be critical consumers of political arguments that cite quantitative evidence. The course will take a handson approach where students learn how to conduct, interpret, and critique quantitative analysis using common statistical software packages.

title: African-American Politics
interdisciplinary_options: African Studies and the African Diaspora
description: The political goals, behavior, voting patterns, group structures, values, and attitudes of various segments of the African-American population, and how these affect the political system.

title: Politics of the United Kingdom
description: This course offers an introduction to politics in the United Kingdom (officially known as the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, and unofficially referred to as Britain).

title: Political Communication
description: The purpose of this course is to explore and examine how elected officials and institutions communicate to the public - and the role journalists and PR professionals play in the process--through readings, seminar, and practicum. The class will challenge students to grapple with questions like - What is the meaning of the "truth" in today's media environment?

title: Political Advocacy
description: Policy-making and implementation in the United States is a complicated process. In any given year laws are passed by elected officials, decisions that guide our implementation of the law are made by government agencies, and rulings handed down by the courts shape our interpretation of those laws.

title: Women in American Politics
description: This course focuses on Women and Politics in the United States. It looks at women's movements both historical and current, how women and men in the United States act differently in politics in terms of voting behavior, as political candidates, and as elected leaders. Students will gain a better understanding of the U.S. political system and how and when women are treated equally and unequally when compared with men.

title: Capitalism and Its Discontents
description: This course analyzes the intersection of capitalist economics and politics from a critical perspective. Capitalism and Marxism will be reviewed, followed by a critical examination of how capitalism has played out in economic/political practice in the world today.

title: Contemporary Political Issues
description: Variable titles. Investigation of selected topics or methods in politics. This course may apply to any of the four areas of the department, and may be repeated depending on subject matter.

title: Contemporary Political Issues: American Politics
description: Investigation of selected topics or methods in American Politics. This course may be repeated depending on subject matter.

title: Contemporary Political Issues: Political Theory
description: Investigation of selected topics or methods in Political Theory. This course may be repeated depending on subject matter.

title: Contemporary Political Issues: Comparative Politics
description: Investigation of selected topics or methods in Comparative Politics. This course may be repeated depending on subject matter.

title: Contemporary Political issues in International Relations
description: Investigation of selected topics or methods in International Relations. This course may be repeated depending on subject matter.

title: Political Justice
description: A study of alternative conceptions of political justice and the attempts to institutionalize them in various political systems.

title: American Political Thought
description: Major political themes from the founding era to the present.

title: Conservatism
description: A survey of modern conservative thought. Attention will be devoted to the theoretical and political cleavages within the Right.

title: Democratic Theory
description: This course examines normative arguments for and against popular rule.

title: Socialism
description: A survey of Marxist and post-Marxist socialist thought.

title: Catholic Political Thought
description: A survey of classic and contemporary political thinkers in the Roman Catholic tradition.

title: Social Science Research Practicum: Cook County Community Survey
description: Familiarity with R, Stata, SAS, or SPSS necessary. Students will analyze this year's iteration of the annual Cook County Community Survey.

title: Politics & the Economy
description: This course examines how and why the U.S. government influences the economy.

title: Crossing Borders: The Politics of Immigration
Politics in Contemporary China
description: This course primarily focuses on domestic politics in post-1978 China.

title: International Relations of Africa
interdisciplinary_options: African Studies and the African Diaspora

title: African Politics
interdisciplinary_options: African Studies and the African Diaspora

title: Women and Politics: A Cross-National Perspective
Fieldwork in Political Science-Internship
description: Practical experience in political and governmental agencies.

title: The Morality and Legality of War
description: The morality and legality of war.

title: Washington DC Internship Seminar
description: Washington DC internship seminar. This course is Writing Intensive.

title: Spanish I
description: This course is an introduction to the basic elements of Spanish language and culture. It is designed for students with no previous experience in Spanish.

title: Spanish for Medical Professionals I
description: This course develops intermediate-level skills in comprehension and conversation for the health professions.

title: Spanish for Medical Interpretation
description: This course is part of an intermediate advanced sequence focusing on advanced oral intercultural skills in Spanish language.

title: Introduction to Critical Analysis in Spanish
description: This course reviews and sharpens the essential skills and techniques necessary to critically read, interpret, and write and speak about all genres of Hispanic literature in Spanish.

title: Introduction to Iberian Literature and Culture
description: An introduction to Iberian literature, film, and cultural production.

title: Introduction to Spanish American Literature and Culture
description: An introduction to Spanish-speaking Latin American literature, film, and other forms of cultural production.

title: Business Spanish
description: Introduction to the fundamentals of the Spanish language and Hispanic culture as it relates to business practices.

title: Literary Criticism
description: Introduction to various theories and methods of literary criticism and textual analysis.

title: Survey of Medieval Literature
description: This course is a survey of the more prominent works of the Spanish Middle Ages.

title: Literary Masterpieces of Spain
description: This course is a survey of representative works of Spanish literature from the Middle Ages to the present.

title: History of Hispanic Feminism
description: This course challenges the usual view of feminism in Hispanic societies.

title: Borges
description: This course focuses on the poetry and prose of Jorge Luis Borges.

title: Spanish Cinema
description: This course focuses on Spanish cinema.
